codeReview.summary: The worker added useful regression coverage for many VAL-EXT negative paths and derivatives fixture metadata, but the feature does not fully satisfy its scoped expected behavior because no automated test was kept for VAL-EXT-010's documented typed empty-state holding-chart contract.

issues.description: This new treasury test only asserts the normal fixture-metadata shape for `/public_treasury/strategy/bitcoin/holding_chart?days=7`; it never verifies the required empty-state branch from VAL-EXT-010 (`{ data: { holdings: [], holding_value_in_usd: [] }, meta: ... }`) even though the feature description explicitly includes documented empty-state behaviors. The transcript shows an attempted empty-state test was abandoned and removed, leaving that contract unguarded.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-refactor-worker skill already says regression-guard features may be test-only when production code satisfies the contract, but the worker still reported this as a procedure deviation and suggested the skill be updated. The skill text may need to be clearer because the worker misread it during this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-refactor-worker/SKILL.md step 4 says 'For regression-guard features, test-only changes are acceptable'; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges repeats that same guidance as if missing.

summary: Review failed: coverage added for many exchange/treasury/onchain guards, but the scoped feature still lacks a retained automated regression for the treasury holding-chart typed empty-state contract (VAL-EXT-010), which the worker explicitly tried and then dropped.
